work_unit: fix W-006 inbox upload path traversal (high severity, audit-derived)
situation: audit-2026-07-03-009 finding W-006: /inbox/upload endpoint accepted arbitrary filename via query param and joined directly to inbox dir (inbox / filename), enabling ../../etc/cron.d/evil style arbitrary file write. Frontend used encodeURIComponent but backend urllib decodes .. back. High severity security hole.
action: Backend: reject filenames containing / \ or ..; take Path.name for safe basename; verify resolved dest stays inside inbox dir (defense in depth against encoding bypass). Frontend: block upload client-side if filename contains path separators or .., with console.warn + alert. 2 files changed, minimal route-fix scope.
outcome_evidence: commit 2c0f878 on auto/opt-web-1782977902 pushed to origin; backend adds 3-layer defense (char filter + Path.name + resolve().relative_to() containment check); frontend adds pre-upload validation with user feedback; 2 files / +20/-2 lines
next_suggestion: 派生 fix #2: W-007 (InboxPage upload 绕过 api.js 包装, medium) — 同 worktree opt-web-1782977902 可继续复用

audit_findings.finding: path traversal: inbox / filename accepts ../.. allowing arbitrary file write
audit_findings.remediation: reject / \ .. chars; take Path.name; verify resolve() stays inside inbox dir
